scope_note: QA Langlands AI Inert Prime Euler Factor cert [409]. Primary sources: Arthur, J. and Clozel, L. (1989) 'Simple Algebras, Base Change, and the Advanced Theory of the Trace Formula' ISBN 978-0-691-08517-3 (automorphic induction, base change for GL_n); Shimura, G. (1971) 'Introduction to the Arithmetic Theory of Automorphic Functions' ISBN 978-0-691-08092-5 (CM forms and Hecke characters); Hecke, E. (1920) doi:10.1007/BF01453601 (L-functions of CM type). CLAIM (narrow and falsifiable): For f=2.2.5.1-125.1-a (GL_2/Q(sqrt5), CM by K=Q(zeta_5)) and prime p≡2 or 3 mod 5 (p inert in Q(sqrt5)/Q, p not 5), the GL_4/Q automorphic induction AI_{Q(sqrt5)/Q}(f) has Euler polynomial P_p^{inert}(Y)=1+p^2*Y^4. All middle coefficients vanish. Derivation (3 steps): (1) CM vanishing: for p inert in Q(sqrt5)/Q, the unique prime P=(p) in Q(sqrt5) is inert in K/Q(sqrt5) because ord_{(Z/5Z)^times}(p)=4 for p≡2,3 mod 5 — hence a_P=0 for f (CM Hecke character vanishes at inert primes of CM extension); (2) GL_2/F local factor at P=(p) with a_P=0 and N(P)=p^2 is Q_P(Z)=1+p^2*Z^2 (Z=p^{-2s}); (3) AI induction at inert p: L_p(Y,AI)=L_P(Y^2,f)=Q_P(Y^2)=1+p^2*Y^4. Structural gap (C4): the naive substitution T=N=0 in the split formula (cert [404]) gives (1+pY^2)^2=1+2pY^2+p^2Y^4 with a_2=2p not 0; the induction formula gives a_2=0. Gap=2p for all inert p. This shows the inert Euler factor is NOT obtained by setting traces to 0 in the split formula — the local structure genuinely differs. Four checks, 22 inert primes p<=200: (C1) p≡2 or 3 mod 5 (22/22 PASS); (C2) palindrome a4=p^2, a1=a2=a3=0 (22/22 PASS); (C3) GL4 Ramanujan: roots of 1+p^2*Y^4 have |root|^4=p^{-2} hence |root|=p^{-1/2} (22/22 PASS); (C4) structural gap inert a2=0 vs naive-split a2=2p, gap=2p>0 (22/22 PASS). Theorem NT: float observer projections (root magnitudes) verify Ramanujan but do not enter QA state. Together [404]+[409] classify all unramified GL_4/Q AI Euler factors for f. Derived 2026-06-13.
